dev_plans.content:
CROSS-SITE GENERALIZATION PROBE (cross-cohort proxy on the existing precomputed splits) — ABSOLUTE-GAP variant.

Available data (do not preprocess from CSV; the precomputed .npz splits are all that exist):
  - {train,val,test}_tfidf.npz (scipy CSR, 10k features)
  - {train,val,test}_meta.npz (keys: 'eth' as a 5-d one-hot, 'labels' as 0/1 mortality)
  - There is NO admission timestamp and NO hospital/ICU-unit ID in this dataset, so a true
    temporal or site split is not available. Use the cross-cohort proxy below instead, and
    document this substitution in prober.py docstring + change_log.

Implementation outline (must follow):
1. At probe init (once), examine test_meta.npz['eth'] (one-hot over white/black/hispanic/asian/other). Pick the ethnicity group with the LARGEST test-set support as the in-distribution reference cohort, and the SECOND-largest group as the shift cohort. Persist the chosen cohort indices to .agent_probe/_cohorts.json and reuse across epochs.
2. Compute symmetric KL divergence between the L1-normalised mean TF-IDF distributions of the two cohorts ONCE at probe init (Laplace smoothing 1e-9). Save to the probe init log.
3. After every training epoch, run the model on the full test split, then compute AUROC on (a) the in-distribution reference cohort and (b) the shift cohort. Both must use sklearn's roc_auc_score on sigmoid probabilities.
4. Per-epoch scalar metric: ABSOLUTE_GAP = abs(AUROC(in-dist) - AUROC(shift)). The metric is non-negative; LOWER is better (cross-cohort parity). This is the canonical robustness direction: a healthy model has a small absolute gap regardless of sign, and standard improvements (regularisation, group-balanced sampling, importance weighting, longer well-tuned training) drive |gap| down.
5. After the final epoch, write .agent_probe/metric/probe_result_<N>.json with keys: metric_name='absolute_auroc_gap_cross_cohort', threshold (numeric, see below), values=[{epoch, value}], stats {min, max, mean, std}, delta=last-first, status, conclusion. PASS iff mean(value over the last 10 epochs) <= threshold; FAIL otherwise. The conclusion string must explain the PASS/FAIL outcome quantitatively.
6. Write .agent_probe/plot/probe_result_<N>.pdf via Plotly (kaleido) with a dashed horizontal threshold line, the per-epoch metric line, threshold-crossing annotation if present, and a stats text box. Reuse the cached y-range from .agent_probe/_axis_range.json across runs (write it the first time, read it thereafter) so plots are visually comparable. Fall back to matplotlib if Plotly export fails.
7. The probe lives entirely in prober.py and is invoked from train.py at the end of every epoch (pass model, val_loader/test_loader, device). Do NOT modify dataset.py or preprocess.py. Do NOT change SEED, BATCH_SIZE, NUM_EPOCHS or the model architecture in train.py beyond what is strictly needed to wire the probe in.
dev_plans.metric: Absolute AUROC gap under cross-cohort shift, per epoch: abs(AUROC(in-distribution reference cohort) - AUROC(shift cohort)), where the cohorts are the largest and second-largest ethnicity groups in the test split. The single PASS/FAIL number is the mean of this per-epoch metric across the last 10 epochs. Lower is better.

dev_plans.content: Statistically rigorous shift-quantification probe combining temporal generalization with explicit covariate-shift estimation and bootstrap confidence intervals. (1) Splits: build three evaluation slices — random_test, temporal_test (last decile by admit time), and (if hospital/ICU-unit ID is present) site_holdout_test (one ICU unit fully held out). (2) Covariate-shift quantification: fit a TF-IDF vectorizer on the training notes (or equivalent feature representation), then at every epoch compute symmetric KL divergence KL_sym(P_train || P_eval) = 0.5*(KL(P_train||P_eval) + KL(P_eval||P_train)) over the top-K (K=5000) token distributions, with Laplace smoothing (alpha=1e-6) to avoid log(0). (3) Importance-weighted AUROC: train a logistic 'domain classifier' (train=0, eval=1) on the TF-IDF features once at the start of training; use its calibrated probabilities to derive density-ratio weights w(x)=p(eval|x)/p(train|x), clipped at the 99th percentile. Compute weighted AUROC on the temporal/site test sets. (4) Per-epoch evaluation: at end of each epoch evaluate model on all three slices; compute AUROC, ECE (Expected Calibration Error, 15-bin), and importance-weighted AUROC. Use 1000 bootstrap resamples to obtain 95% CIs on each AUROC. (5) Composite metric per epoch: shift_robustness_score = AUROC(temporal_test) / AUROC(random_test). This is logged via a TensorBoard SummaryWriter alongside CIs. (6) Artifacts: TensorBoard scalars, per-epoch JSON with full statistics, end-of-training PDF report containing reliability diagrams for each slice, AUROC-with-CI line plots, and a KL-divergence trajectory plot. (7) Statistical test: a DeLong test compares random_test and temporal_test AUROCs each epoch; p<0.01 with score below threshold triggers a 'distribution shift confirmed' record in the report.
dev_plans.metric: shift_robustness_score = AUROC(temporal_test) / AUROC(random_test), computed per epoch as a float in (0, ~1.05]
dev_plans.threshold: 0.96 — corresponds to a relative AUROC retention of 96%, equivalent to ~0.03 absolute drop when random AUROC is ~0.85 (typical MIMIC mortality benchmark). Finlayson et al. 2021 and Nestor et al. 2019 report well-tuned clinical models retaining 0.94-0.98 of in-distribution AUROC under one-year temporal shift, so 0.96 is a calibrated, achievable healthy boundary rather than an aspirational target.

dev_plans.content: Tooling/library-based implementation leveraging mature distribution-shift libraries (Alibi Detect, Evidently AI, scikit-learn). (1) Dependencies: install alibi-detect (for MMDDrift, ChiSquareDrift, ClassifierDrift), evidently (for prebuilt data-drift dashboards), and scikit-learn for the base AUROC/calibration utilities. (2) Pre-training setup: build splits identical to plan A (random + temporal + optional site). Initialize an alibi_detect.cd.MMDDrift detector fit on training-set embeddings (last hidden layer of the model, or TF-IDF features pre-training) with p_val=0.05 and a permutation backend. Initialize an evidently DataDriftPreset report against training data as the reference. (3) Hook into pipeline: write a PyTorch Lightning Callback (or Keras callback) named DistributionShiftMonitor with on_validation_epoch_end. Each epoch the callback (a) extracts model embeddings on a fixed sample of 2000 random_test and 2000 temporal_test rows; (b) calls MMDDrift.predict() and records the MMD statistic and p-value; (c) computes AUROC on both slices via sklearn.metrics.roc_auc_score; (d) logs to MLflow/W&B via lightning's self.log API. (4) End-of-epoch composite metric: temporal_generalization_index = (AUROC_temporal - 0.5) / (AUROC_random - 0.5), the ratio of above-chance lift retained on the shifted slice. Logged per epoch. (5) Weekly/end-of-training: generate the full evidently HTML data-drift report and a Fairlearn MetricFrame breakdown by ethnicity using fairlearn.metrics.MetricFrame on the temporal slice, attaching both as W&B artifacts. (6) Alerts: a callback hook checks if the metric is below threshold; if so, emits a Slack/webhook notification (configurable) and writes the offending epoch's evidently report path to a 'shift_alerts.log' file. (7) Output artifacts: MLflow runs with per-epoch scalar metric, evidently HTML reports, fairlearn subgroup tables, MMD p-value time-series plot, and a final markdown summary auto-generated from W&B.
dev_plans.metric: temporal_generalization_index = (AUROC_temporal - 0.5) / (AUROC_random - 0.5), recorded once per epoch as a float
dev_plans.threshold: 0.92 — measures the fraction of above-chance discriminative power preserved under temporal shift. With typical random-split AUROC ~0.85 and acceptable shifted AUROC ~0.82, the index lands near 0.91-0.94 for healthy MIMIC-trained models in the literature. 0.92 is therefore a realistic, well-tuned-run-achievable line that still flags meaningful degradation when crossed.
